title: Deliciously Dense Indian Potatoes Dosa
description: A rich and tender Rajasthani dish featuring crispy potatoes in a dosa form, perfect for a lazy Sunday morning, this satvik-friendly recipe is a twist on traditional Indian cuisine, with a crispy exterior and fluffy interior, made with a variety of ingredients and cooked to perfection on a slate-tile vessel.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the sliced potatoes and chopped onion, and let it sit for 10 minutes to allow the potatoes to release excess moisture.
In a blender or food processor, combine the grated ginger, minced garlic, and curry leaves, and blend until you get a smooth paste.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the rice flour, lentil flour, and salt, and mix well.
Add the blended ginger-garlic paste to the flour mixture, and mix until well combined.
Add the water to the flour mixture, and mix until you get a smooth and thick batter.
Heat a non-stick skillet or slate-tile vessel over medium heat, and brush it with a little oil.
Using a ladle, pour a small amount of the batter onto the skillet or vessel, and spread it evenly to form a dosa.
Arrange a few slices of the potato-onion mixture on one half of the dosa, and fold the other half over to cover it.
Cook the dosa for 2-3 minutes on each side, until it is crispy and golden brown.
Repeat the process with the remaining batter and potato-onion mixture.
Serve the dosas hot with your favorite chutney or sambar.
